Value: Crispy Rice Cakes Press and Fry
Double Handle Cheese Knife, Fryer, Hakuzushi Press
Sheet trays, hotel pans, cutting board
Unit: gr, Batch x1: 908.0, Batch x2: 1816, Batch x3: 2724, Batch x4: 3632, Prep/Brand: prepped
Unit: tt, Batch x1: nan, Batch x2: 0, Batch x3: 0, Batch x4: 0, Prep/Brand: Pam Aerosol Oil Spray
Using a scale, measure out balls/pyramids of rice 2# at a time
Using the large hakuzushi box press, add rice to the bottom in an even layer.
Using the top of the press, press rice into a firm cake that is even in thickness and shape.
Reserve to sheet trays in the cooler, uncovered for ~24 hours near the fans.
If reserving past ~24 hours, cover with a lexan lid to prevent over drying.
Using the double handled cheese knife, cut the cakes into 2x6cm bars.
Reserve to hotel pans to fry, stored in cooler until needed.
Fry cakes in small batches in ~350°F fryer, until edges are lightly toasted. Cool in basket while other batches cook.
Reserve to hotel pans and store in cooler
